auditors.finding: Canon and older repo precedent allow support/open-file ideas in principle, but the web design UI still lacks a bounded server-owned open-reference contract.

The correct contour-level verdict is deferred pending a reference contract: support/open-file actions are canonically acknowledged, but browser path submission, direct file truth, parsing, and private path disclosure remain forbidden.

The initial artifact set had a closeout-resilience defect: the closeout claimed closure while Git fields were still pending and the resume path was not exact. The closeout was corrected before commit.

evidence.fact: Open Logs, Open State, Open Registry, Open Data Folder, and Open Diagnostics Bundle are acknowledged as operator support actions and not primary truth sources.
Diagnostics bundles are support snapshots, and logs may be opened for humans but must not be parsed as API.
Older support/open-file actions resolve fixed targets server-side and open them without parsing them into truth.
Independent audit previously confirmed support/open-file actions do not parse files and are not used as truth inputs.
Browser-submitted bundle_path and log_path payloads are explicitly rejected in the web design UI action surface.
Current route evidence support result still carries raw evidence_path, so it is not yet a safe bounded open-reference contract.
A prior admission contour already deferred show-in-file-manager behavior because web contour lacked a safe server-owned open-location contract.

checks.reason: The web design UI still lacks a dedicated bounded reference-id contract, and evidence_path is still path-shaped in current support results.
Artifacts keep state, registry, logs, and data-folder opening out of current web admission.
Artifacts preserve the no-path browser payload rule.
Artifacts preserve support-only semantics and forbid truth derivation from opened material.
Post-artifact audit found and the final artifact set fixed the closeout status/Git/resume mismatch before commit.
